concerns.text: The plan fits joint regression models with crossing number (c) and braid index (b) as separate predictors, yet the braid index is mathematically constrained by b ≤ c (a definitional inequality). This violates the independence assumption required for meaningful coefficient interpretation; VIF will be high by design and the coefficients cannot be interpreted as independent effects. Using such a model to claim ‘predictive relationship’ is scientifically unsound.

concerns.location: plan.md:Section 3 – Precision Establishment for Core Invariants
concerns.text: Precision of the braid index is validated by comparing Knot Atlas values to KnotInfo reference values. However, Knot Atlas and KnotInfo often derive from the same underlying knot tables, so this cross‑check is not independent. The validation therefore becomes circular, providing no genuine evidence of measurement accuracy.

Residual analysis aims to identify hyperbolic knot families that deviate from the fitted trend. Because the predictors are not independent, any systematic deviation may simply reflect the mathematical constraint (b ≤ c) rather than a meaningful ‘family effect’, making the residual‑family interpretation potentially misleading.
